key_insight: A physics-informed residual neural network can learn hot deformation and dynamic recrystallization behavior of a Mo-rich Ti alloy while staying consistent with constitutive mechanics.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable constitutive models for thermomechanical process design, helping predict flow stress and DRX during hot working of titanium alloys.
method: Built two stacked residual physics-informed neural networks (STAR-PINNs) for flow-stress prediction.
Added thermomechanical constraints such as strain hardening, thermal softening, strain-rate sensitivity, and post-peak softening via automatic differentiation.
Extended one model with DRX-aware dual outputs, JMAK-Avrami regularization, DRX saturation constraints, and Arrhenius consistency.
evidence_signal: The DRX-aware STAR-PINN achieved RMSE 11.69 MPa, MAE 4.83 MPa, R^2 0.9850, and cross-validated RMSE 12.47 ± 0.26 MPa on experimental data from 800–1050 °C and 0.01–10 s^-1.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of experimental training data, so extrapolation outside the tested temperature and strain-rate range may be unreliable.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Residual Deep Learning for Constitutive Modeling of Hot Deformation and Dynamic Recrystallization in a Mo-Rich $α+β$ Titanium Alloy
